attacking_style: Operates from breakaways rather than bunch sprints. Tactically manages energy expenditure to survive climbs while preserving sprint capacity for final kilometers.
team_tactics: Insufficient data to establish clear team tactical patterns. Appears to operate independently within breakaway contexts.
terrain_preference: Hilly stages with selective climbs that thin breakaways but don't eliminate his sprint advantage. Vulnerable on sustained steep gradients but capable of bridging short gaps in closing kilometers.
vulnerability: Gets dropped on final climbs when pace is sustained, relying on tactical hesitation from stronger climbers to bridge back. Pure mountain stages would expose climbing limitations.
key_patterns: Accepts being dropped on climbs, then bridges when rivals hesitate (2019 Giro evidence)
Prioritizes energy conservation over breakaway cooperation to maintain sprint advantage (both Giro appearances)
Targets intermediate points and secondary objectives from breaks (2017 KOM collection, 2019 sprint win)
Exploits tactical indecision among stronger climbers in final kilometers to salvage position
form_trajectory: unknown: only 2019 data available, showing smart tactical execution but insufficient recent observations
